name: flashcms
summary: Puck editor renders in the parent DOM (iframe disabled) so imported stylesheets apply directly. Fixes blocks looking plain/unstyled in the admin preview.
notes: Snapshot cut from the sfh-group-website working codebase.
See docs/extending.md for the pattern to add tables/blocks/routes.

changes: PageEditor now passes `iframe={{ enabled: false }}` to <Puck>. The default iframe often fails to inherit parent stylesheets, leaving preview blocks unstyled. Disabling the iframe makes globals.css + puck-preview.css apply directly.
INSTALL Step 9a: adopt Next.js multiple-root-layouts pattern — move public routes into app/(frontend)/ and delete the shared root app/layout.tsx so admin chrome and public chrome don't nest.
No template code changes — pure docs.
Template's PageEditor.tsx now imports `@/app/globals.css` by default — matches the flat layout most Next.js projects use.
Docstring lists both common paths (flat vs route-group).
INSTALL.md Step 9b documents adjusting this import per-project.
lib/known-routes.ts entries now carry `section` and `icon` fields; the /admin/pages listing derives grouping and glyphs from the data instead of a hardcoded sectionFor() switch.
Template ships a minimal starter list (home + about) so a fresh install no longer inherits SFH-group's routes.
New knownSectionOrder() helper returns unique sections in first-appearance order.
INSTALL.md Step 9 documents the section/icon fields.
lib/site.ts now reads SITE_SLUG from env (falls back to 'sfhgroup' so existing installs keep working).
scripts/seed.ts reads SITE_SLUG / SITE_NAME / SITE_DOMAIN so one script can bootstrap any tenant.
Enables installing flashcms into multiple Next.js projects that share one Postgres — pages, posts, media, and analytics are already foreign-keyed to sites.id.
Add TopProgress client component — global link/form click listener that shows a red progress bar at the top of the viewport during route transitions and clears when the new pathname lands.
Add loading.tsx skeleton fallback in app/(admin)/admin/(authed) so slow segment fetches render a shimmer instead of freezing the current UI.
Add busy cursor + prefers-reduced-motion fallback and 15s safety timeout so the bar never sticks.
Includes Puck page editor, TipTap posts, media library, users + roles, analytics (visits + Web Vitals), SEO, site-settings, in-CMS update flow, and a full admin UI.
